description: Heatmap of the change in grade-level proficiency (all subjects and grades) relative to each SBE region's own 2019 pre-COVID level, by region (rows) and year (columns), 2018 to 2025, on a diverging scale anchored at the 2019 baseline: rust means below 2019 (down to about −17 points in 2021), pale teal means at or just above it (no region exceeded 2019 by more than about 1 point). The 2019 column is grey by construction (the baseline). The 2020 column is absent because state testing was waived during COVID; every region's 2021 cell is deep rust, fading toward the grey baseline by 2025 to show partial, incomplete recovery.

title.text: Proficiency dipped everywhere after COVID — recovery is partial
subtitle: Change in grade-level proficiency (GLP, all subjects and grades) vs. each region's 2019 baseline, in
percentage points, 2018–2025. Rust = below 2019; the grey 2019 column is the baseline. No 2020 — testing waived.

usermeta.source: Source: NCDPI accountability test scores (EOG/EOC grade-level-proficient composite), 2018–2025. 2020 omitted — state testing waived. Style-guide demo.
